expectations.text: Output contains a properly structured search matrix with Context, Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 sections
expectations.evidence: All three tiers present with proper markdown table headers and a Context section with Goal, Type, Domain

expectations.text: Matrix uses at least 3 different engine categories (e.g., MDN, GitHub, Google, caniuse)
expectations.evidence: Uses MDN/caniuse, GitHub, Google, StackOverflow, W3C/IETF, YouTube, and Hacker News

expectations.text: Search queries use engine-specific operators such as language:, stars:>50, or site: filters
expectations.evidence: Includes language:go, language:rust, stars:>20, stars:>15, site:developer.mozilla.org, site:caniuse.com, site:web.dev, votes:3

expectations.text: Tier 2 queries broaden scope compared to Tier 1, and Tier 3 explores alternative or lateral sources
expectations.evidence: Tier 1 targets core implementations and benchmarks; Tier 2 adds Node.js, migration guides, and firewall concerns; Tier 3 uses W3C specs and community sources

expectations.text: Each row includes specific acceptance criteria for evaluating search results
expectations.evidence: All 11 rows have an Acceptance column with specific criteria

expectations.text: Output includes a Runtime Recovery section with fallback strategies
expectations.evidence: Runtime Recovery section contains 4 bullet points with concrete alternatives for each failure mode

expectations.text: Output includes a Grading Summary section with per-tier pass/fail thresholds
expectations.evidence: Grading Summary table lists thresholds for Tier 1 (3/4), Tier 2 (2/4), Tier 3 (1/3), and Overall (6/11)

expectations.text: At least one query targets browser compatibility or feature detection data
expectations.evidence: Tier 1 row 1 explicitly targets MDN and caniuse for browser compatibility with version-level acceptance criteria

expectations.text: At least one query targets latency or throughput benchmarks comparing WebTransport and WebSocket
expectations.evidence: Tier 1 row 4 queries for 'WebTransport vs WebSocket benchmark latency throughput comparison'

expectations.text: Matrix includes queries for server-side WebTransport implementations in at least 2 languages
expectations.evidence: Tier 1 covers Go and Rust, and Tier 2 covers Node.js, giving 3 languages total, but the Rust query acceptance threshold is low (stars:>15 yields marginal repos)
